fills.note: Standard Ruth pilot BCD ref per convention §5.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"Scene 3 (vv.19-22) shifts to INTIMATE per MM Section 1; within it Naomi's blessing at v.20 lifts to CEREMONIAL. Narrator framing at v.17, v.18, v.23 stays INFORMAL_CASUAL.","scene_level":[{"scene_id":"S3","override_value":"INTIMATE"}],"moment_level":[{"verse":"2:20","override_value":"CEREMONIAL"}]}
fills.note: Encoded the two register shifts the MM marks explicitly: INTIMATE over the whole mother/daughter exchange, CEREMONIAL at the v.20 blessing.

Two mints (PROVISION_BROUGHT_HOME, REDEEMER_RECOGNITION) — no approved arc token fit the grain-home beat or the redeemer-naming turn. RECOGNITION_EXCHANGE was the near-miss for the redeemer beat but reads as person-recognition (the 'Is this Naomi?' kind), not kinship-role recognition.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM arc: 'The passage moves from the field to home... carries it into town... hands her the food left over' — a day's gleaning brought home as provision; no approved arc token covers this.

MM arc: 'Naomi blesses him by YHWH... and tells Ruth the man is near to them, one of their redeemers' — the redeemer-role recognized for the first time in the book; the central turn, with no approved token for it.

fills.note: Enumerated every context kind the §2.2 prose invokes: field-day carry-forward (prior pericope/action), audience knowing since 2:1 (audience-knowledge), kinsman-redeemer institution, kinship, locations, harvest temporal, and YHWH invoked (divine).

Field line plain (CHRONICLE), intimate mother/daughter talk (WARM), feeling quickens into the blessing (RISING), then the long calm close (STILLED), ending in settled hope with a kinsman in view (ANTICIPATORY).
Steady field work, pace rises at home, settles into calm counsel, then v.23 widens weeks of harvest into one line.
Closes the provision strand and Ruth's clinging; opens the redeemer thread; reactivates the hesed word (last at 1:8); stages the floor for the next chapter's plan.
Narrator names Ruth plainly; no marked reference.
No approved scene_kind covers a narrated field-work record. Minted GLEANING_SCENE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM S1: 'Ruth gleans in the field until evening, beats out what she gathered, and it comes to about an ephah' — a narrated gleaning/work scene with no approved scene_kind fit.

fills.note: Append per gap hint: B13 referenced via the field being his portion; B3 only weakly referenced via Ruth's kinship — reviewer may prefer to drop B3 from S1.
MM gives no Significant Absence block for S1 (only a 3F). Filling null rather than inventing one; reviewer's call whether the un-credited abundance counts as a load-bearing absence.
Function toward Naomi here is the providing daughter-in-law; pairs with B3 as MOTHER_IN_LAW.
Ruth named plainly; unmarked.
MM marks this explicitly: 'named here only by the kinship-word, her mother-in-law.' Treated as free-form referential value per the worked pair's idiom.

The leftover 'from her satiety' is the leftover roasted grain from Boaz's meal (2:14); FIG_0104 closes the 'had leftover' verb here. Assigned O10 (Roasted Grain); reviewer may prefer a distinct leftover code.
No approved scene_kind fits the carrying-home-and-handing-over beat. Minted.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM S2: 'Ruth lifts the grain and comes into the town. Her mother-in-law sees what she gleaned. Then Ruth brings out and gives her what she had left over' — bringing provision home; no approved fit.

fills.note: MM marks it: 'her mother-in-law through the exchange; named Naomi at v.20 and v.22.' Free-form referential value.
MM marks the narrator's foreigner-epithet returning at v.21 (FIG_0001). Used the registry form for B9.
MM marks the progression: 'the man who took notice of you' before the name, then named, then 'one of our redeemers.' Free-form value capturing the unnamed-then-named move.
YHWH named by the divine name; no specially marked form flagged in this pericope.
First naming of the redeemer-role in the book (CB_0001); MM calls explicit attention to it. Free-form referential value.
No approved role_in_scene_being for the household dead held within hesed. Minted.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM S3 3A: 'the dead of the household... those the blessing keeps inside the reach of hesed' — Naomi's dead husband and sons, named only as 'the dead'; no approved role fits.

fills.note: MM significant-absence dwells on this: 'The dead are named only as the dead, not as Elimelech, Mahlon, and Chilion.' Free-form value.

The map keeps this collective deliberately uncoded ('the dead' = B2, B4, B5). No registry B code exists; proposed a descriptive id pending registry decision. The individual dead are also appended to S3 as REFERENCED (B2/B4/B5).
The 'young men/young women' report-variation is captured in P13's slots (report_variation_form) rather than as a referential form here.

BLESSING_SCENE was the near-miss (the v.20 blessing is the ceremonial peak), but the MM 3F frames the scene's spine as the redeemer recognition at the name. Minted REDEEMER_RECOGNITION_SCENE; reviewer may prefer BLESSING_SCENE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM S3 3F: 'The turn of the passage: at the name Boaz, Naomi recognizes a redeemer' — a dialogue scene whose spine is the kinship-role recognition; no approved scene_kind fits.

fills.note: Append per hint: the dead husband and sons, referenced within 'the dead' / hesed over the living and dead.
Dominant function in S4 is gleaning/staying-close through the harvest.
S4 names her plainly 'her mother-in-law' with no map-attention 'Referential form' line; unmarked.
MM 3F: 'Closes the pericope and the season in one quiet line.' Approved token.
No approved proposition_kind for hand-threshing/beating-out (CB_0041). Minted.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P2: 'beating out | who beat it out? Ruth | beat out what? what she had gleaned' (chavat, CB_0041); no approved proposition_kind covers threshing.

fills.note: No approved proposition_kind for the yield amounting to a measure. Minted.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P3: 'measuring | how much was it? about an ephah | an ephah of what? barley' (CB_0040); no approved proposition_kind for a quantity/measure result.

fills.note: yield_exactness APPROXIMATE preserves the MM's 'about an ephah' (the same spare-measure idiom as the ~ten years in P01).
P4 carries two micro-actions (lifted/took up, came into town); modeled with a components array and ARRIVED_AT as the head kind.
Decomposed the 'lifted it / came into town' pair into a components array per the worked-pair idiom.
Avoided forbidden 'recipient' key. FIG_0104 (abundance triplet) closes and FIG_0113 (leftovers after satiety) opens on this gift.
Information-seeking question; speech_act carries the act.
Preserved the doubled 'where gleaned / where worked' question form.
Blessing of the still-unnamed man (recipient B13 under the form 'the man who took notice'); WISHES_FOR_THIRD_PARTY.
Ruth answers by naming the man — identification of Boaz.
Speech answering Naomi's question (P7).
The CEREMONIAL-register blessing by name; invoked_divine_agent per worked-pair idiom.
Echoes the first (unnamed) blessing P8; triggered by hearing the name P9.
Declaration about hesed continuing the blessing.
Preserved the MM's deliberately-open antecedent (YHWH's or the man's hesed) and the living-and-dead pairing (CB_0039).
Naomi identifies the man as near-kin / a redeemer.
First naming of the redeemer-role in the book (CB_0001); FIG_0112 Close-to-Us.
Recognition flows from hearing the name (P9).
Ruth reports Boaz's prior instruction; speech_act carries the report.
Captured the MM's load-bearing report-variation (Boaz's 'young women' at 2:8 reported as 'young men') and the returning Moabite-epithet (FIG_0001).
Intimate address form per worked-pair idiom (_INTIMATE suffix).
Naomi's counsel answers Ruth's report (P13).
The devek/clinging verb (CB_0018, FIG_0012) — Ruth staying close to the young women.
Preserved both named harvest seasons (CB_0026, season-frame opened at 1:22 closes here).
She follows Naomi's counsel (P14).
'And she lived with her mother-in-law.'
Closing line; left unlinked (the still floor for the next chapter).

Granularity held at map level. Only P4 was decomposed internally (home_bringing_components: TOOK then ARRIVED_AT) to preserve the 'lifted it / came into the town' pair. Flags re-anchor cleanly by the existing verse anchors.

remarks: Mints declared: arc_element PROVISION_BROUGHT_HOME & REDEEMER_RECOGNITION; scene_kind GLEANING_SCENE, PROVISION_HOMECOMING_SCENE, REDEEMER_RECOGNITION_SCENE; role_in_scene_being DECEASED_KIN; proposition_kind THRESHED & MEASURED. All justified against MM substance with no approved fit. Key judgment flags for the reviewer: (1) S3 scene_kind — chose REDEEMER_RECOGNITION_SCENE over the approved BLESSING_SCENE because the MM frames recognition as the scene's spine; (2) the household dead (S3 entry5) given a proposed descriptive being_id THE_DEAD_HAMETIM since the map keeps the collective uncoded — B2/B4/B5 are also appended individually as REFERENCED; (3) S2 leftover assigned O10 (leftover roasted grain from 2:14, where FIG_0104 closes) — reviewer may want a distinct code; (4) S1 significant_absence left null (MM gives no block; did not invent); (5) referential_form values treated as free-form per the worked pair and not declared as axis mints — marked only where the MM's prose explicitly calls attention (S2/S3 B3, S3 B9/B13/B18/the-dead).
